meta.title: Code Concentration Issues Report
meta.summary: A report listing files where multiple responsibilities are mixed into a single file or where code is duplicated, along with identified problems and suggested improvements.

severity.CRITICAL: Immediate refactoring required — 5+ responsibilities mixed in one file, or a single method exceeding 100 lines.
severity.WARNING: Refactoring recommended — 2–4 responsibilities mixed, or duplicate code exists across files.
severity.INFO: Monitor — currently within acceptable limits, but splitting should be considered if the file grows further.

responsibilities: Display user grade, total assets, and trade count
Dividend history: HTTP fetch + WebSocket subscription
Chzzk channel URL parsing → stock registration API call (handleAddStock)
Holdings list rendering with profit/loss rate calculation

problems.detail: Four independent features — nickname editing, dividend history, stock registration, and portfolio display — are all crammed into a single component.
handleAddStock() directly handles URL object parsing, channelId extraction, API request, and error branching — roughly a 40-line inline block.
The holdings rendering logic (profit rate calculation based on avgPrice) is nearly identical to the holdings useMemo in HomeView.tsx.
9 useState calls: nameOverride, isEditingName, nameInput, nameUpdating, dividendHistory, dividendHistoryLoaded, addUrl, addStatus, addMsg.

suggestedRefactor: ProfileStockList.tsx — Holdings list component (shareable with HomeView)
DividendHistory.tsx — Dividend history section
AddStockForm.tsx — URL parsing + stock registration form (or extract logic into a useAddStock hook)
ProfileHeader.tsx — User grade display + nickname editing

responsibilities: Total return rate and grade calculation
Holdings list with profit/loss rate calculation

problems.detail: Home dashboard, ranking, real-time trade feed, and order history popup are all bundled into one component.
The profit rate calculation logic inside the holdings useMemo is nearly identical to the holdings rendering in ProfileView.tsx.
liveTrades, recentlyViewedIds, and onRemoveRecent — states that do not belong exclusively to HomeView — are passed down from App.tsx.

suggestedRefactor: LiveTradesFeed.tsx — Real-time trade feed section
HoldingsSummary.tsx — Holdings summary (shareable with ProfileView)
useHoldings.ts — Shared hook for profit rate calculation
OrderHistoryModal.tsx — Order history popup as a standalone component

responsibilities: DB transaction processing (simultaneous updates to User, UserShare, Stock, and Order)

problems.detail: executeMarketOrder() spans ~120 lines, handling price calculation, validation, DB persistence, cache update, and broadcast all in one place.
Three ConcurrentHashMaps are declared as direct service-class fields. Any change to the cache strategy requires modifying TradeEngine itself.
userLocks / stockLocks management is inlined inside submitTrade(). Debugging a potential deadlock is very difficult in this structure.
Price calculation, balance/share validation, DB persistence, cache synchronization, and WebSocket notification are all mixed into a single private method.

suggestedRefactor: TradeCache.java — In-memory cache management for balances and shares (evict, load, update methods)
PriceImpactCalculator.java — Exponential price impact formula (static utility or separate component)
TradeValidator.java — Pre-trade validation for balance, share quantity, and total supply
Split executeMarketOrder() internals into: calculatePrice(), validate(), persistTrade(), broadcastTrade()

responsibilities: Register a new stock + calculate listing price

problems.detail: HttpClient and ObjectMapper are declared as StockService fields, and fetchChzzkChannelInfo() handles everything from making the HTTP request to parsing the JSON response.
getEnvOrProperty() has identical logic to ChzzkLivePollingService.getEnv() — including .env file path resolution and quote stripping.
HttpClient.newBuilder().connectTimeout(Duration.ofSeconds(5)).build() is declared identically in ChzzkLivePollingService.

suggestedRefactor: ChzzkApiClient.java — Consolidate both OpenAPI (channel info) and private API (live status) HTTP calls into one client
EnvResolver.java or @Value / @ConfigurationProperties — eliminate .env parsing duplication
ListingPricePolicy.java — Extract the listing price calculation for easier testability

responsibilities: Stream state transition handling (started / ongoing / ended)

problems.detail: getEnv() has identical logic to StockService.getEnvOrProperty() — both services contain their own copy of .env parsing.
The HttpClient.newBuilder() initialization block is identical to the one in StockService.
pollLiveStatus() handles state transition decisions, pre-stream snapshots, dividend triggering, and WebSocket broadcast all at once — making unit testing extremely difficult.
fetchChannelStatus() relies on an undocumented api.chzzk.naver.com endpoint with cookie-based authentication (NID_AUT / NID_SES) — migration to the official OpenAPI should be considered.

suggestedRefactor: ChzzkApiClient.java — Merge with StockService's HTTP client to eliminate duplication
EnvResolver.java — Centralize environment variable resolution
LiveStatusHandler.java — Extract stream state transition logic (started / ongoing / ended)
Split pollLiveStatus() internals into: fetchStatuses(), handleTransition(), broadcastChanges()

problems.detail: tick() acts as a simple scheduler but internally contains the full broadcast logic that iterates over all stocks and all intervals.
The buildOneMin → aggregate → fillGaps pipeline is used by both getCandles() and tick(), but all methods are private, making them impossible to unit test externally.

suggestedRefactor: CandleAggregator.java — Extract buildOneMin / aggregate / fillGaps as pure functions (easily unit testable)
CandleBroadcaster.java — Move the @Scheduled tick logic into a dedicated class

responsibilities: Guest → Google account linking (linkWithPopup + credential-already-in-use handling)
Initial live trade feed loading + WebSocket subscription

problems.detail: handleGuestLogin() inlines fingerprintjs dynamic import, custom token exchange, and anonymous → custom-token sign-in transition — roughly 20 lines of logic.
The credential-already-in-use recovery flow — storing pendingGuestMerge in localStorage and re-authenticating — is implemented directly in App.tsx at ~25 lines.
liveTrades and recentlyViewedIds are view-specific states that have been lifted all the way up to App.tsx, causing unnecessary props drilling.

suggestedRefactor: useAuth.ts — Extract all login / logout / account-linking / guest-login logic into a custom hook
useLiveTrades.ts — Move liveTrades state and WebSocket subscription into a dedicated hook
useRecentlyViewed.ts — Move recentlyViewedIds state into a dedicated hook

responsibilities: MegaphoneModal component definition (co-located in same file)
Megaphone item card + purchase eligibility check
Real-time WebSocket subscription for incoming megaphone posts

problems.detail: MegaphoneModal is defined as a separate function at the top of ShopView.tsx, making the file 280 lines. Splitting it into its own file would reduce each file to ~130 lines.

suggestedRefactor: MegaphoneModal.tsx — Extract modal into its own component file
MegaphonePostList.tsx — Extract recent megaphone post list (optional)

crossCuttingIssues.title: Chzzk API client duplication
crossCuttingIssues.detail: HttpClient initialization, ObjectMapper, and .env parsing logic (getEnvOrProperty / getEnv) are declared identically in both files.
crossCuttingIssues.fix: Consolidate into a single ChzzkApiClient.java containing both OpenAPI (channel info) and private API (live status) methods, with environment variable resolution centralized in EnvResolver.

crossCuttingIssues.title: Holdings profit rate rendering duplication
crossCuttingIssues.detail: Profit rate calculation based on avgPrice and the holding card rendering logic are nearly identical in both files.
crossCuttingIssues.fix: Centralize the calculation logic in a useHoldings(portfolio, streamers) hook and extract a shared HoldingCard.tsx component.

crossCuttingIssues.title: .env file parsing logic duplication
crossCuttingIssues.detail: Both classes contain nearly identical logic for resolving values by checking system environment variables first, then falling back to frontend/.env file parsing — including the same fallback path search and quote-stripping behavior.
crossCuttingIssues.fix: Extract into an EnvResolver.java utility class, or replace the whole approach with Spring @Value / application.properties loading.

prioritizedActionPlan.reason: The fastest backend improvement — eliminates duplicate code across two services by adding new files only, with minimal impact on existing code.
A 120-line single method is the hardest point to debug and test. Splitting into calculatePrice(), validate(), persistTrade(), and broadcastTrade() dramatically improves readability.
Eliminates the ProfileView / HomeView duplication so that any future change to profit rate calculation only needs to happen in one place.
Moving all auth logic from App.tsx into a hook reduces App.tsx to under 100 lines and makes the login flow unit-testable.
Extract nickname editing, dividend history, and stock registration each into their own component to reduce the 396-line file.
The smallest-scope, lowest-risk refactoring — just move the modal into its own file.
